title: Complete math assignment with 10 problems
description: Read chapter 4 and solve the 10 problems at the end of the chapter. Ensure all work is shown for partial credit.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Get your textbook and a clean sheet of paper.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Read chapter 4 carefully before solving.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Work through each problem carefully.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Check your math before submitting.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Submit your completed assignment.

microtasks.text: Find chapter 4 in the book
Open to the problems page
Get a clean sheet of paper
Have a pencil and eraser ready
Read the first 2 paragraphs slowly
Note any new formulas

title: Write English essay draft
description: Write a 500-word draft about the themes in Macbeth. Focus on ambition and guilt.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Review your notes on Macbeth.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Identify the main themes: ambition and guilt.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Write the full draft without stopping to edit.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Read through your draft once.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Fix spelling and grammar errors.

microtasks.text: Look in your notebook or folder
Find the section on themes
Write down 'Ambition' and 'Guilt'
Note one example for each theme
Write one sentence about what Macbeth is
Write your thesis statement

title: Prepare Presentation
description: Build a 10-slide presentation on a chosen topic. Focus on clear structure, visuals, and confident delivery.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Choose your topic and gather reference material.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Identify the 3 key messages you want to communicate.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Design slides with clear headings, bullet points, and visuals.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Run through the presentation aloud at least once.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Export or save final version and practise delivery.

microtasks.text: Open a blank document or note
Write one sentence describing the topic
Write: who will watch this?
Note what they already know about the topic

subtasks.text: Find 3 reliable sources on the topic

microtasks.text: Open a search engine or library database
Bookmark or copy 3 useful pages

subtasks.text: Note down key facts and quotes
Open presentation tool (PowerPoint or Google Slides)
Read through all slides for errors
Save or export the final file

title: Study for Exam
description: Review all relevant chapters, create a summary sheet, and practise past questions before the exam.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Collect all notes, past papers, and the syllabus.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Identify the high-priority topics likely to appear.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Actively study each topic using summaries and practice.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Test yourself with past questions under timed conditions.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Rest well the night before. No new material.

subtasks.text: Collect notes from all relevant chapters

microtasks.text: Open your notebook or digital notes
List the chapter numbers that are in scope

subtasks.text: Find at least one past exam paper

microtasks.text: Check the school portal or textbook back pages
Save or print the paper
Write each topic on a separate line
Mark which ones feel weakest

subtasks.text: Read notes for topic 1 and summarise in your own words
Create a one-page cheat sheet of key points
Attempt 5 questions without looking at notes
Mark your answers and note mistakes
Re-study any topics you got wrong
Skim through your cheat sheet one more time
Pack everything you need for exam day

title: Complete Coding Assignment
description: Plan, write, test, and submit a coding assignment. Break the problem into small functions and test each one.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Read the brief carefully and clarify requirements.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Plan your approach before writing any code.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Write the code function by function, testing as you go.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Test edge cases and clean up the code.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Write comments, zip or submit, and confirm submission.

subtasks.text: Read the full assignment instructions once

microtasks.text: Open the brief document or PDF
Highlight the key requirements

subtasks.text: List what the program must do

microtasks.text: Write out the inputs the program receives
Write out the expected outputs
Draw boxes for each major function
Write one line describing each function

subtasks.text: Identify which language features to use
Set up project files and folder structure
Build remaining functions one at a time
Run the program with sample input
Test edge cases (empty input, large numbers)
Add comments to explain each function
Zip or package the project files
Upload to the submission portal and confirm

title: Organise Study Notes
description: Sort, clean up, and structure all your notes so they are easy to find and use during revision.

sections.heading: Preparation
sections.content: Gather notes from every subject in one place.

sections.heading: Understanding
sections.content: Decide on a folder or labelling system.

sections.heading: Execution
sections.content: Sort, rewrite unclear notes, and fill gaps.

sections.heading: Review
sections.content: Check each section is complete and readable.

sections.heading: Completion
sections.content: Back up digital notes or file physical ones safely.

subtasks.text: Find all physical notebooks and loose papers

microtasks.text: Check your bag, desk, and shelf
Stack everything in one pile

subtasks.text: Open digital notes apps and export if needed

microtasks.text: Open each app (Notion, Google Docs, etc.)
List any documents that need organising

subtasks.text: Label each pile or folder by subject

microtasks.text: Write subject names on sticky notes or tabs
Place each note into the correct pile

subtasks.text: Discard or archive notes no longer needed
Identify pages that are messy or incomplete
Rewrite key points clearly and neatly
Place physical notes in a binder or folder
Save digital notes to cloud storage
Confirm everything is accessible and readable
